ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Timestamp of when the metrics were recorded */,
  "queen" INTEGER /* Identifier for the specific queen or job manager */,
  "threads" INTEGER /* Number of threads used in the computation */,
  "solutions" INTEGER /* Number of solutions attempted during computation */,
  "seconds" INTEGER /* Time taken for the computation in seconds */,
  "microseconds" INTEGER /* Microseconds part of the total computation time */,
  "cpu_temp" REAL /* CPU temperature during the task in Celsius */,
  "gpu_temp" REAL /* GPU temperature during the task in Celsius */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job associated with the metrics */,
  "node_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the specific computing node */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., CPU usage, GPU usage, temperature) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., Celsius, seconds, MB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the job */,
  "job_name" TEXT /* Name of the job */,
  "submission_time" INTEGER /* Time when the job was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the job (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "job_start_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the job started */,
  "job_end_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the job ended */,
  "job_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the job in seconds */,
  "job_priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the job */,
  "job_resources" TEXT /* Resources allocated to the job (e.g., number of CPUs, GPUs, memory) */,
  "job_output" TEXT /* Path to the output file or log of the job */,
  "job_error" TEXT /* Path to the error log of the job */,
  "job_environment" TEXT /* Environment variables or settings used during the job execution */,
  "job_script" TEXT /* Path to the script or command used to run the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role assigned to the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "created_at" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the user has admin privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert generated (e.g., high CPU temp) */,
  "alert_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "alert_resolution_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "alert_resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on how the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Timestamp for the resource usage recorded */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage during the time */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* Percentage of GPU usage during the time */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Amount of memory used in MB */,
  "node_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the specific computing node */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Amount of disk space used in MB */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network bandwidth usage in MB/s */,
  "energy_usage" REAL /* Energy consumption in kWh */,
  "environment_temperature" REAL /* Ambient temperature in Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_usage_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES system_health ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_health" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health check */,
  "check_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the health check was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Overall status of the system (e.g., healthy, warning, critical) */,
  "cpu_health" TEXT /* Health status of CPU */,
  "gpu_health" TEXT /* Health status of GPU */,
  "node_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the specific computing node */,
  "memory_health" TEXT /* Health status of memory */,
  "disk_health" TEXT /* Health status of disk */,
  "network_health" TEXT /* Health status of network */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption in kWh */,
  "environment_temperature" REAL /* Ambient temperature in Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency record */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job that has dependencies */,
  "dependent_job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job that this job depends on */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., data, resource) */,
  "dependency_description" TEXT /* Description of the dependency */,
  "dependency_status" TEXT /* Status of the dependency (e.g., met, unmet) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_dependencies_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_dependencies_dependent_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job related to this log */,
  "log_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the log was created */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message logged for this job execution */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log message (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log message (e.g., system, user) */,
  "log_context" TEXT /* Contextual information related to the log message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_logs_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the action performed */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., job, user, system) */,
  "action_target" TEXT /* Target of the action (e.g., job ID, user ID) */,
  "action_result" TEXT /* Result of the action (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "login_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the user logged in */,
  "logout_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the user logged out */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  "session_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the user logged in */,
  "session_device" TEXT /* Device used for the session (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "session_browser" TEXT /* Browser used for the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
